source.roadArticle: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Scenic Byway

attribution.text: Route, junction, and history details adapted from the Wikipedia article "Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Scenic Byway", by Wikipedia contributors, licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0.

sources.name: Federal Highway Administration — National Scenic Byways

gallery.cap: National Scenic Byway Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad (Maryland) N
Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway A Foreboding Sunset over Bla
gallery.desc: A dark, cloud-tainted sunset over the mysterious waters of the Blackwater National Wildlife Refuge evokes a feeling of isolation similar to what someone trying to traverse the landscape at nightfall might feel. Location: Location: Blackwater National Wildlife Refuge, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo Courtesy of Dorchester County Tourism

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway A Greensboro Landscape
gallery.desc: The farm field creates a wide green space somewhere between summer's cultivation and winter's fallow. Location: Location: Greensboro, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo by Kathleen Mackel

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway An Autumn draped Tuckahoe Cr
gallery.desc: Fallen leaves grace the river bank scene along the Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway. Location: Location: Tuckahoe Crossing, Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo by Kathleen Mackel

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Bucktown Village Store All T
gallery.desc: The quaint yellow-painted village store sits quietly on the roadside. Today visitors can tour the restored, 19th-century store, rent bicycles and kayaks, and enjoy guided tours of the historic landscapes Location: Location: Bucktown, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo courtesy of Bucktown Village Foundation

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Cambridge Fleet at Long Whar
gallery.desc: A clear blue sky and high thin clouds reflect on the smooth waters of the Choptank River while five white-washed hulls lay still as their wooden masts await the next sail. Location: Location: Long Wharf, Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Caroline County Courthouse D
gallery.desc: A flag flies before the impressive white pillars of the Caroline County Courthouse on a cloudless summer day. Location: Location: Caroline County Courthouse Square, Denton, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photograph by Ellis Davison

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Cars Parked Before Buildings
gallery.desc: Restaurants, shops, art galleries, museums, and accommodations line Race Street in Historic Downtown Cambridge. The town's architecture and infrastructure remain largely intact. Visitors and locals can stroll the sidewalks, grab a bite to eat, and enjoy the small town pace of life. Location: Location: Cambridge, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo Courtesy of Dorchester County Tourism

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Church Creek Farmland
gallery.desc: In Church Creek, a red barn sits far off the road, across a field of yellow soy plants ready for harvest. Location: Location: Church Creek, Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Dorchester County Courthouse
gallery.desc: An interpretive sign on the grounds of the Dorchester County Courthouse marks important moments in Underground Railroad history. Location: Location: Dorchester County, Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Entering James Webb's Cabin
gallery.desc: A leafy branch hangs over James Webb's rustic log cabin, which a white-haired man enters. Location: Location: Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Heart of Chesapeake Country
gallery.desc: Vernetter Pinder, a Harriet Tubman re-enactor, sings gospel songs to a group of on-lookers at the Dorchester County Visitor Center. Location: Location: Cambridge, Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Leverton Safe House
gallery.desc: The lush but short crops surrounding Jacob Leverton's safe house would have offered little coverage for Underground Railroad travelers attempting to find sanctuary. Location: Location: Maryland Status: Public domain. Caroline Office of Tourism

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway On the Mason and Dixon Line
gallery.desc: A nest of green vegetation surrounds the stone marking the Mason and Dixon Line. Location: Location: Maryland (39.695° N 76.119° W) Status: Public domain. Caroline Office of Tourism

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway On the Road to East New Mark
gallery.desc: A tree-lined country road on a hazy summer day leads into the Town of East New Market's National Historic District. Location: Location: East New Market, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo by Linda Cashman

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway On the Road to Poplar Neck
gallery.desc: The road to Poplar Neck, an unpaved country lane, is lined with black leaf-barren trees, creating an elegant scene. Location: Location: Maryland Status: Public domain.

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Pooled Water at the Greensbo
gallery.desc: A rocky, tree-lined bank holds in the Choptank's rippling waters at a crossing known as Red Bridges. Location: Location: Red Bridges, Greensboro, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo by Kathleen Mackel

gallery.cap: Harriet Tubman Underground Railroad Byway Running Through Mt. Pleasant
gallery.desc: Among the graves of the Mt. Pleasant Cemetery, a Harriet Tubman re-enactor flees through the headstones and wet grass to freedom. Location: Location: Mt. Pleasant, Maryland Status: Public domain. Photo by Melissa Grimes Guy
